settings.name: Theme Demo
settings.__comment_settings: Global fallback theme is 'default'. Category/group overrides below will demonstrate theme inheritance.

categories.name: Ocean Category (applies to all groups below unless overridden)
categories.__comment: This category sets theme=ocean. Groups that don't specify a theme will inherit ocean.

jobs.name: Theme should be forest
jobs.command: echo This group's theme is 'forest' (group-level override)
jobs.__comment: Group overrides category, so theme=forest when this group is selected

groups.name: No Theme Group (inherits from category)
groups.__comment: This group has no theme field — inherits 'ocean' from parent category

jobs.name: Theme should be ocean (from category)
jobs.command: echo This group inherits theme 'ocean' from the category
jobs.__comment: No group-level theme, so falls back to category's 'ocean'

categories.name: Cyberpunk Category
categories.__comment: Entirely different theme for this category and its groups

jobs.name: Theme should be bumblebee
jobs.command: echo Group overrides category with 'bumblebee' theme

categories.name: No Theme Category (falls back to settings)
categories.__comment: This category has no theme field — falls back to settings.theme = 'default'

groups.name: Group inherits from category (which falls back to settings)

jobs.name: Theme should be 'default' (from settings block)
jobs.command: echo No category theme, no group theme → falls back to settings.theme='default'

jobs.name: Theme should be warm_pastels
jobs.__comment: Even though category has no theme, group explicitly defines one

jobs.name: Theme: lavender (still, no job-level theme)
jobs.command: echo Still lavender because job doesn't override theme (not supported at job level)

jobs.name: Theme: golden_lavender
jobs.command: echo This demonstrates a less common theme name

groups.name: No Theme Group (inherits sunflower from category)
